AU_14574.prompt:
TASK: From the provided soldier fatality record, identify the best-estimate GPS coordinates for the place of death. YOU MUST also extract and include the predominant physical location/place names found in the text — this is a MANDATORY part of the output whenever location details exist.

PROCESS:
  1. FIRST, check the authoritative_ai_override field.  If it contains coordinates or a grid reference, use those as your starting point — but STILL search for and include location/place names.
  2. Analyse all location-related fields, including:
     - detailed_location_description
     - grid_reference
     - map_sheet_number_utm_zone
     - military_operation
     - narrative summary
     - any place names (village, hamlet, district, province)
     - any fire support bases, rivers, roads, or landmarks
     - any ARVN/US/Australian operational areas
  3. Identify ALL location names in the text — villages, hamlets, districts,
     provinces, FSB names, LZ names, rivers, rubber plantations, landmarks.
  4. If a grid reference MGRS / UTM / partial coordinate appears anywhere in the text, extract it exactly as written.
  5. Convert any full or partial grid reference into an approximate GPS coordinate when possible.
  6. If no grid reference exists, use:
     - Vietnam War 1:50,000 map sheets
     - historical village/hamlet locations
     - known AO boundaries
     - documented operations to estimate the lowest-level location (village > hamlet > district > province).
  7. Always output one single best-estimate GPS coordinate in decimal degrees.

LOCATION NAME RULES (MANDATORY when location info exists):
  - Extract ALL predominant physical place names found in the text.
  - Include: villages, hamlets, districts, provinces, rivers, mountains, roads,
    rubber plantations, landmarks, FSB names, LZ names.
  - Do NOT omit location names just because you have GPS coordinates.
  - Concatenate place names from smallest to largest (e.g. "Ap Lo Gom, Phuoc Tuy").
  - Only omit location names if absolutely NO location info exists in the text.

OUTPUT FORMAT (MANDATORY):
Return a single line:
  "best_estimate_gps": "LAT, LON [GRID1, GRID2, ...] — location_names"

CRITICAL: The " — location_names" suffix is MANDATORY whenever any place
name, landmark, FSB, LZ, village, hamlet, district, province, river, road,
or rubber plantation is mentioned in the source text. DO NOT return bare
GPS coordinates if location details exist.

Rules:
  - If grid fragments exist → include them in [brackets] before the location.
  - If no grid fragments exist → output GPS then location names.
  - If no estimate is possible → return an empty string.

Examples:
  "best_estimate_gps": "16.533, 107.533 — Ap Lo Gom, Phuoc Tuy Province"
  "best_estimate_gps": "10.512, 106.345 [YS478548] — Courtenay Rubber, FSB Flinders, Phuoc Tuy"
  "best_estimate_gps": "11.221, 108.112 [48PYS1234] — near Song Rai River, Phuoc Tuy"
Return ONLY the output line — no commentary, no markdown, no explanation.

The SOURCE TEXT to analyse is provided below between the <source> tags.
This text is compiled from the record's ai_response and authoritative_ai_override fields.

<source>
{
  "2_unit_served_with": "Australia, Australian Army, Australian Army Training Team Vietnam",
  "service_status": "Regular",
  "operation": "Battle of Nam Dong",
  "operational_setting": "Camp McBride at Nam Dong, a Civilian Irregular Defense Group (CIDG) camp in a secluded valley 50 kilometres west-north-west of Da Nang, was manned by South Vietnamese personnel, an Australian AATTV adviser (WO2 Conway), and US Special Forces soldiers. Its primary role was border surveillance. Just before 3:00 am on 6 July 1964, a Viet Cong battalion of 800-900 guerrillas launched a coordinated attack on the camp involving mortars and small arms fire. WO2 Conway and US Master Sergeant Gabriel Alamo dashed 30 metres to a mortar pit inside the inner perimeter to repel the fierce assault.",
  "cause_of_death": "Killed in Action. Shot in the head and mortally wounded by enemy fire after entering a mortar pit during the Viet Cong assault on the camp. He died of his wounds approximately 30 minutes later, becoming the first Australian combat casualty of the Vietnam War.",
  "grid_reference": "NOT_DOCUMENTED_IN_RECORD",
  "map_sheet": "NOT_DOCUMENTED_IN_RECORD",
  "location_description": "Camp McBride, Nam Dong CIDG camp, Thua Thien Province, South Vietnam.",
  "movements_48h": "WO2 Conway was stationed in garrison at Camp McBride, Nam Dong. In the 48 hours prior to the attack, tensions were high due to local Viet Cong activity (including the execution of two village chiefs on 5 July) and internal conflict among the camp's defenders, leading the camp commander to increase the number of guards and stock fighting positions with extra ammunition.",
  "sources": [
    "Australian War Memorial (AWM) Last Post Ceremony Transcript (C2846663)",
    "Virtual War Memorial Australia (VWMA) records for CONWAY, Kevin George",
    "AWM Roll of Honour"
  ],
  "others_in_incident": "US Master Sergeant Gabriel Alamo (KIA in the same mortar pit). During the wider Battle of Nam Dong, one additional US Special Forces soldier and 57 ARVN/CIDG defenders were also Killed in Action.",
  "burial": "Initially interred in Mac Dinh Chi Cemetery in Saigon. Transferred in October 1964 to Ulu Pandan Cemetery in Singapore, and reinterred in Kranji War Cemetery in 1975. On 2 June 2016, his remains were repatriated to Australia and he was laid to rest with full military honours in Cleveland Cemetery, Queensland (Plot 14, Row B, Grave 9).",
  "track_or_patrol": "NOT_DOCUMENTED_IN_RECORD",
  "probable_grid": "16.1175°N 107.678°E (Geographic coordinates for Nam Dong)",
  "notes": "High confidence for operational setting, cause of death, and burial details based on detailed historical records from the AWM and VWMA. The exact military map sheet and MGRS grid references are not explicitly documented in the cited summary texts, correctly invoking the NOT_DOCUMENTED_IN_RECORD fallback.",
  "references": [
    "https://vwma.org.au/explore/people/654274",
    "https://www.awm.gov.au/collection/C2846663"
  ]
}
</source>

Extract the requested information based on your system instructions from the source text above.
AU_14574.response: "best_estimate_gps": "16.1175, 107.678 — Camp McBride, Nam Dong CIDG camp, Thua Thien Province, South Vietnam"
